title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre union workers in Brookline voted to authorize a strike due to stalled contract negotiations.
The strike authorization follows six months of slow negotiations after management's voluntary union recognition in June 2024.
Union focuses on job protection, aiming to negotiate issues like part-time worker rights and self-service kiosk implementation.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts outfielder Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs' 2026 projections do not see any Red Sox player exceeding 20 home runs, including Abreu.
Abreu hit 22 homers in 115 games last season and shined during the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are projected to select Arizona State tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Max Iheanachor, originally from Nigeria, earned Second Team All-Big 12 honors as a tackle at Arizona State.
Patriots eye Iheanachor as a potential long-term replacement for 35-year-old veteran tackle Morgan Moses.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials denounce racial comments after South High's basketball game against Andover, prompted by online video.
NAACP and YWCA condemn threats and racist remarks towards South High players; call for community action.
The social media video criticizing South High players has been removed, initial video had over 2 million views.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, was arrested for allegedly sexually exploiting four girls aged 12 to 16.
Authorities found child sexual abuse material on Mota's phone during a luggage search at Logan Airport in June 2025.
Mota admitted to receiving CSAM from approximately 20 to 25 minors and paying them via Apple Cash and Cash App.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened Thursday after a water main break caused extensive flooding.
A 12-inch diameter pipe ruptured, flooding streets and affecting traffic and local businesses, including three daycares.
Repairs and road restoration were completed by Thursday afternoon after continuous work by crews for 48 hours.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, indicted on three rape counts and 17 indecent assault charges involving 11 patients in Framingham.
Alleged assaults occurred between 2021 and 2023 at Charles River Medical Associates, where Todd worked as a rheumatologist.
Over 200 patients joined a class action lawsuit against Todd after accusations of unnecessary examinations surfaced.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul advises using a second battery for a RAM van converted to a camper, suggesting lithium iron phosphate for reliability.
A voltage inverter and potentially a rooftop solar panel can aid power needs when converting a van to a camper.
Van converters should calculate their wattage needs, with many managing on 1,000–2,000 watts for appliances and electronics.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was initially arrested in August 2025, charged with trafficking for sexual servitude, and held on $25,000 bail.
Prosecutors dismissed the remaining charge as the victim invoked the Fifth Amendment, leading to a lack of prosecution.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old were arrested for allegedly robbing an elderly woman at gunpoint in Charlestown.
The suspects allegedly approached the victim on Adams Street, stole her purse, and fled towards Monument Square, police said.
Witnesses helped police identify the suspects, who were arrested after a short pursuit; Jordan faces multiple charges and no bail.
